evaluations.query: Add loading states to a dashboard that shows user profile cards fetched from an API
expected_behavior: Uses skeleton placeholders matching card shape instead of a generic spinner
Applies animate-pulse class for subtle shimmer animation on skeleton elements
Shows skeleton only after 200ms delay to avoid flash for fast loads
Matches skeleton count to expected card count in the grid layout

evaluations.query: Implement an infinite-scrolling product list that works for screen reader users
expected_behavior: Uses role='feed' on the list container with aria-busy during fetches
Provides a visible 'Load more' button fallback below the IntersectionObserver sentinel
Announces item count changes via aria-live='polite' in a visually hidden region
Sets aria-posinset and aria-setsize on each article element in the feed

evaluations.query: Build a sortable task list where users can reorder items by dragging
expected_behavior: Uses @dnd-kit/core with both PointerSensor and KeyboardSensor registered
Supports arrow key navigation with Enter or Space to pick up and drop items
Announces drag state changes via aria-live='assertive' for screen readers
Provides visual feedback during drag with opacity or shadow changes on the item

evaluations.query: Create a toast notification system that shows success and error messages
expected_behavior: Uses role='status' with aria-live='polite' for success toasts and role='alert' for errors
Auto-dismisses success and info toasts after 5 seconds but never auto-dismisses errors
Positions toasts top-right on desktop and bottom-center on mobile using media queries
Includes a visible dismiss button on every toast, not relying only on auto-dismiss

evaluations.query: Design a settings page that has basic profile fields plus advanced options like API keys and webhooks
expected_behavior: Groups settings by frequency: common fields always visible, advanced behind disclosure
Uses native <details>/<summary> elements for accordion sections instead of custom ARIA
Limits visible options to 5-7 items per group following Miller's Law
Never hides primary actions behind disclosure — only secondary and advanced options

evaluations.query: Build a tab bar for a dashboard with 12 dynamically-generated category tabs
expected_behavior: Uses role='tablist' with role='tab' on each button and aria-selected for active tab
Shows scroll arrow buttons only when tabs overflow the container width
Implements roving tabindex: active tab gets tabIndex=0, all others get tabIndex=-1
Supports arrow key navigation between tabs with Home/End for first/last tab

evaluations.query: Show product details when a user clicks on a product in a list
expected_behavior: Uses a drawer or side panel instead of a modal for browsable detail views
Preserves the product list context visible alongside the detail panel
Returns focus to the clicked product item when the drawer is closed
Provides Escape key to close the drawer and a visible close button

evaluations.query: My infinite scroll feed shows a loading spinner forever when the API returns an empty array on the first page
expected_behavior: Detects and renders an empty state illustration or message when the first page returns zero items
Does not keep the IntersectionObserver sentinel active when there are no more items to load
Distinguishes between loading state and empty result state in the component logic
Provides a clear call-to-action in the empty state instead of a blank or stuck screen

evaluations.query: My drag-and-drop sortable list works on desktop but on mobile the drag gesture conflicts with native page scrolling
expected_behavior: Configures a touch activation constraint like delay or distance threshold to distinguish drag from scroll
Uses the TouchSensor with activationConstraint from dnd-kit to require a deliberate hold before drag starts
Does not suggest disabling native scroll or using touch-action none on the entire page
Provides a visible drag handle that limits the touch target for initiating drag operations

evaluations.query: Our toast notifications display error messages returned directly from the API response body
expected_behavior: Warns against rendering raw API error messages in the UI without sanitization
Recommends mapping API error codes to predefined user-friendly messages instead of displaying raw server text
Does not suggest using dangerouslySetInnerHTML for toast content
Advises stripping or escaping HTML from API responses before displaying in toast notifications
